task_completion.description: Verify that the screenshot shows the GCompris application running 'The fifteen game'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris user interface should be visible, including typical GCompris menu elements and the sliding tile puzzle activity.

task_completion.description: Verify that the game is currently on Level 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The level indicator (usually a progress bar or dots in the GCompris UI) should show that the 3rd level is active.

task_completion.description: Verify that the puzzle consists of a 4x4 grid.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There should be a 4x4 grid layout for the tiles, accommodating 15 numbered tiles and 1 empty space.

task_completion.description: Verify that the puzzle is completely solved in the correct order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tiles must be arranged in row-major order: Row 1 has [1, 2, 3, 4], Row 2 has [5, 6, 7, 8], Row 3 has [9, 10, 11, 12], and Row 4 has [13, 14, 15] with the bottom-right corner being the empty space.

task_completion.description: Verify that the congratulations/bonus animation is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A win state animation, specifically the GCompris flower bonus animation, should be visible on the screen indicating the puzzle was successfully completed.

integrity.description: Ensure the screenshot is of the actual GCompris application and not a different 15-puzzle game or web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI must match GCompris's distinct visual style, including its specific icons, toolbars, and window borders.

integrity.description: Ensure the background matches the expected level environment for Level 3.
integrity.visual_evidence: The background behind the puzzle tiles should be a Fishing Boat Scene (Fishing_Boat_Scene.svg), which is specific to levels 1-4 of this activity.

integrity.description: Ensure the screenshot was not generated by opening a pre-made image or using an image editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no image editing software (like GIMP) or image viewer UI visible. The screenshot should capture the live game.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is /home/ga/Documents/fifteen_solved.png. The Fifteen Game in GCompris uses a fixed 4x4 grid. Level 3 displays numbers on the tiles (odd levels show numbers) and uses a fishing boat scene background. The solved state requires tiles 1-15 in row-major order: row 1=[1,2,3,4], row 2=[5,6,7,8], row 3=[9,10,11,12], row 4=[13,14,15] with the blank space at the bottom-right corner. Solving the puzzle triggers a flower bonus/congratulations animation.
